html: Once TCRO clicks, the next question from any CFO is simple: “What will it take to do this here?”
text: Once TCRO clicks, the next question from any CFO is simple: “What will it take to do this here?”

html: This is the practical checklist. It is written for finance leaders who want to move from understanding Total Cost of Risk Ownership (TCRO) to running it as a decision standard across maintenance, capital, and insurance – without turning it into a multi-year science project.
text: This is the practical checklist. It is written for finance leaders who want to move from understanding Total Cost of Risk Ownership (TCRO) to running it as a decision standard across maintenance, capital, and insurance – without turning it into a multi-year science project.

html: For the TCRO fundamentals, see Total Cost of Risk Ownership vs Cost of Risk.
text: For the TCRO fundamentals, see Total Cost of Risk Ownership vs Cost of Risk [link to /blog/total-cost-of-risk-ownership].

html: Step 1: Define the scope and set a TCRO target
text: Step 1: Define the scope and set a TCRO target

html: Before touching systems, get alignment on:
text: Before touching systems, get alignment on:

html: Scope. Are you starting with one flagship asset, a campus, or a specific asset class (for example, seniors or office)?
text: Scope. Are you starting with one flagship asset, a campus, or a specific asset class (for example, seniors or office)?

html: Time horizon. Are you planning to operate TCRO as a one-year pilot, a three-year program, or a permanent standard?
text: Time horizon. Are you planning to operate TCRO as a one-year pilot, a three-year program, or a permanent standard?

html: Target. Do you want to reduce TCRO by a specific percentage, stabilize it, or improve its predictability?
text: Target. Do you want to reduce TCRO by a specific percentage, stabilize it, or improve its predictability?

html: Practical actions:
text: Practical actions:

html: Document the current view of risk costs (claims, premiums, downtime, emergency spend, wage premiums) – even if it is imperfect.
text: Document the current view of risk costs (claims, premiums, downtime, emergency spend, wage premiums) – even if it is imperfect.

html: Align your executive team on why TCRO matters and what questions it should answer that Cost of Risk cannot.
text: Align your executive team on why TCRO matters and what questions it should answer that Cost of Risk cannot.

html: A short pre-read pointing to TCRO vs Cost of Risk and The TCRO Dashboard: What Your Board Needs to See, and What It Does Not helps anchor this.
text: A short pre-read pointing to TCRO vs Cost of Risk and The TCRO Dashboard: What Your Board Needs to See, and What It Does Not helps anchor this [link to /blog/tcro-dashboard-board-needs].

html: Step 2: Build an initial TCRO model with your own assumptions
text: Step 2: Build an initial TCRO model with your own assumptions

html: Next, build a first-pass TCRO model, even if your data is not fully ready. The goal is directionally right, not perfect.
text: Next, build a first-pass TCRO model, even if your data is not fully ready. The goal is directionally right, not perfect.

html: Actions:
text: Actions:

html: Use the TCRO calculator to plug in your best estimates for:
text: Use the TCRO calculator to plug in your best estimates for:

html: Direct losses and claims
text: Direct losses and claims

html: Downtime and business interruption
text: Downtime and business interruption

html: Premiums and retentions
text: Premiums and retentions

html: Operating and maintenance inefficiency
text: Operating and maintenance inefficiency

html: Governance and reporting overhead
text: Governance and reporting overhead [link to /tcro#calculator]

html: Run a few simple scenarios:
text: Run a few simple scenarios:

html: “Status quo” vs “fewer failures” vs “better insurance terms.”
text: “Status quo” vs “fewer failures” vs “better insurance terms.”

html: This gives you:
text: This gives you:

html: A baseline TCRO estimate for your current state.
text: A baseline TCRO estimate for your current state.

html: A sense of which components are most material – and therefore where to focus implementation first.
text: A sense of which components are most material – and therefore where to focus implementation first.

html: Step 3: Inventory assets and risk drivers for the pilot scope
text: Step 3: Inventory assets and risk drivers for the pilot scope

html: TCRO only becomes real when it is tied to specific systems and buildings.
text: TCRO only becomes real when it is tied to specific systems and buildings.

html: For your chosen scope:
text: For your chosen scope:

html: Build a risk-aware asset inventory:
text: Build a risk-aware asset inventory:

html: Critical systems and equipment (mechanical, electrical, water, gas, life safety).
text: Critical systems and equipment (mechanical, electrical, water, gas, life safety).

html: Age, condition, replacement cost, and business importance.
text: Age, condition, replacement cost, and business importance.

html: Map known risk drivers:
text: Map known risk drivers:

html: Historical failures and near-misses.
text: Historical failures and near-misses.

html: Outbreaks or environmental incidents (in seniors and care environments).
text: Outbreaks or environmental incidents (in seniors and care environments).

html: High-friction maintenance areas and repeated tenant complaints.
text: High-friction maintenance areas and repeated tenant complaints.

html: This inventory is the starting point for both From Reactive to Predictive and How TCRO Changes Capital Planning.
text: This inventory is the starting point for both From Reactive to Predictive and How TCRO Changes Capital Planning [links to /blog/reactive-to-predictive-maintenance-real-estate and /blog/tcro-capital-planning].

html: Step 4: Put data governance in place for the pilot
text: Step 4: Put data governance in place for the pilot

html: Before layering on more data or AI, decide how you will govern what you already have.
text: Before layering on more data or AI, decide how you will govern what you already have.

html: For the pilot scope:
text: For the pilot scope:

html: Assign data owners for:
text: Assign data owners for:

html: Asset inventory
text: Asset inventory

html: Incidents and failures
text: Incidents and failures

html: Work orders and maintenance
text: Work orders and maintenance

html: Claims and insurance
text: Claims and insurance

html: Outage and wage premium records (if applicable)
text: Outage and wage premium records (if applicable)

html: Standardize definitions and coding:
text: Standardize definitions and coding:

html: What counts as an incident or failure?
text: What counts as an incident or failure?

html: How are outages, outbreaks, and surge staffing recorded?
text: How are outages, outbreaks, and surge staffing recorded?

html: How are events linked to assets and locations?
text: How are events linked to assets and locations?

html: Make sure there is one common data environment – even if it is simple – where these data sets meet.
text: Make sure there is one common data environment – even if it is simple – where these data sets meet.

html: You can use Data Governance Is Not an IT Project. It Is the Operating System of Financial Control as the reference point for this step.
text: You can use Data Governance Is Not an IT Project. It Is the Operating System of Financial Control as the reference point for this step [link to /blog/data-governance-real-estate].

html: Step 5: Instrument the highest-impact risks
text: Step 5: Instrument the highest-impact risks

html: With governance in place, you can add the missing visibility without over-engineering.
text: With governance in place, you can add the missing visibility without over-engineering.

html: For the pilot scope, focus on:
text: For the pilot scope, focus on:

html: Systems where failures have caused the most damage or disruption.
text: Systems where failures have caused the most damage or disruption.

html: Environments where risk has the highest human consequence (seniors, public venues).
text: Environments where risk has the highest human consequence (seniors, public venues).

html: Assets where a single failure could move TCRO meaningfully.
text: Assets where a single failure could move TCRO meaningfully.

html: Practical actions:
text: Practical actions:

html: Deploy targeted monitoring (vibration, temperature, flow, air quality, gas) on those systems.
text: Deploy targeted monitoring (vibration, temperature, flow, air quality, gas) on those systems.

html: Integrate signals with your work-order system so alerts trigger real workflows, not just dashboards.
text: Integrate signals with your work-order system so alerts trigger real workflows, not just dashboards.

html: Ensure every intervention is logged and tied back to the original signal.
text: Ensure every intervention is logged and tied back to the original signal.

html: This is where From Reactive to Predictive: Why Your Maintenance Model Is Now a Finance Strategy becomes your operating manual.
text: This is where From Reactive to Predictive: Why Your Maintenance Model Is Now a Finance Strategy becomes your operating manual [link to /blog/reactive-to-predictive-maintenance-real-estate].

html: Step 6: Connect actions and outcomes back into TCRO
text: Step 6: Connect actions and outcomes back into TCRO

html: Now you have three pieces: a TCRO model, governed data, and live signals with workflows. The next step is to close the loop.
text: Now you have three pieces: a TCRO model, governed data, and live signals with workflows. The next step is to close the loop.

html: For the pilot period (for example, 6–12 months):
text: For the pilot period (for example, 6–12 months):

html: Track events and near-misses where:
text: Track events and near-misses where:

html: A signal led to an intervention that prevented or reduced a failure.
text: A signal led to an intervention that prevented or reduced a failure.

html: A failure still occurred despite interventions.
text: A failure still occurred despite interventions.

html: For each, estimate:
text: For each, estimate:

html: Direct repair or claim cost that was avoided or reduced.
text: Direct repair or claim cost that was avoided or reduced.

html: Downtime and wage impact that was avoided or reduced.
text: Downtime and wage impact that was avoided or reduced.

html: Effect on premiums or retentions, where applicable.
text: Effect on premiums or retentions, where applicable.

html: Periodically recalculate TCRO for the pilot scope and compare:
text: Periodically recalculate TCRO for the pilot scope and compare:

html: “Before TCRO and predictive operations” vs “After.”
text: “Before TCRO and predictive operations” vs “After.”

html: This gives you hard numbers you can share with:
text: This gives you hard numbers you can share with:

html: Your board, using a simplified TCRO dashboard.
text: Your board, using a simplified TCRO dashboard [link to /blog/tcro-dashboard-board-needs].

html: Your broker and insurers, as in Insurance Is No Longer a Fixed Line Item.
text: Your broker and insurers, as in Insurance Is No Longer a Fixed Line Item [link to /blog/data-driven-insurance-renewals-real-estate].

html: Step 7: Use TCRO to drive capital planning and insurance – then scale
text: Step 7: Use TCRO to drive capital planning and insurance – then scale

html: Once you have evidence from the pilot, you can start making TCRO-informed decisions at scale.
text: Once you have evidence from the pilot, you can start making TCRO-informed decisions at scale.

html: Actions:
text: Actions:

html: Feed pilot results into your next capital cycle:
text: Feed pilot results into your next capital cycle:

html: Prioritize projects that generate the biggest TCRO reduction per dollar.
text: Prioritize projects that generate the biggest TCRO reduction per dollar.

html: Defer projects that are capital-intensive but TCRO-neutral.
text: Defer projects that are capital-intensive but TCRO-neutral.

html: Bring TCRO data to your next insurance renewal:
text: Bring TCRO data to your next insurance renewal:

html: Show how continuous monitoring and interventions have reduced expected loss.
text: Show how continuous monitoring and interventions have reduced expected loss.

html: Work with your broker to reflect this in structure and pricing.
text: Work with your broker to reflect this in structure and pricing.

html: Decide how to extend:
text: Decide how to extend:

html: Another asset class (for example, from seniors to mixed-use).
text: Another asset class (for example, from seniors to mixed-use).

html: Another geography or campus.
text: Another geography or campus.

html: Additional risk domains (for example, ESG/TCRO, labor/TCRO).
text: Additional risk domains (for example, ESG/TCRO, labor/TCRO).

html: From here, TCRO is no longer a model. It is part of how your portfolio is run.
text: From here, TCRO is no longer a model. It is part of how your portfolio is run.

html: Where to go from here
text: Where to go from here

html: If you are ready to start TCRO implementation, you can choose a path that fits how you prefer to work:
text: If you are ready to start TCRO implementation, you can choose a path that fits how you prefer to work:

html: Get a one-day pilot estimate for a flagship asset. Use Estimate Your Savings to share one representative property and receive a reviewed estimate, within one business day, of what TCRO-aligned predictive operations and risk data could change for that asset.
text: Get a one-day pilot estimate for a flagship asset. Use Estimate Your Savings to share one representative property and receive a reviewed estimate, within one business day, of what TCRO-aligned predictive operations and risk data could change for that asset [link to /tcro/estimate-your-savings].

html: Model the pilot scope yourself first. Use the TCRO calculator to scope a small, realistic pilot – one building or campus – and see how changes in failures, downtime, and premiums would move TCRO. Then use Talk to an Expert to pressure-test that pilot plan and align it with your board and insurance strategy.
text: Model the pilot scope yourself first. Use the TCRO calculator to scope a small, realistic pilot – one building or campus – and see how changes in failures, downtime, and premiums would move TCRO [link to /tcro#calculator]. Then use Talk to an Expert to pressure-test that pilot plan and align it with your board and insurance strategy [link to your contact modal or /contact].

html: With a defined pilot, a clear checklist, and the right partners, TCRO implementation becomes less about theory and more about a controlled, measurable shift in how you manage risk, capital, and the buildings people rely on every day.
text: With a defined pilot, a clear checklist, and the right partners, TCRO implementation becomes less about theory and more about a controlled, measurable shift in how you manage risk, capital, and the buildings people rely on every day.

html: Which piece of this checklist feels most challenging in your organization right now – alignment on TCRO at the exec level, data governance, or picking the right pilot scope?
text: Which piece of this checklist feels most challenging in your organization right now – alignment on TCRO at the exec level, data governance, or picking the right pilot scope?
